title: Third-Party App Access
spl:
index=gws sourcetype="gws:token" event_name="authorize"
| stats dc(actor.email) as unique_users by app_name, scope
| sort -unique_users
description: OAuth app grants to third-party applications create data access risks. Monitoring enables governance and risk assessment.
value: OAuth app grants to third-party applications create data access risks. Monitoring enables governance and risk assessment.
implementation: Ingest token audit logs. Track OAuth grants by application and scope. Identify high-risk scopes (full Drive access, Gmail read). Alert on new third-party apps accessing sensitive scopes. Report for governance review.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Google Workspace token audit log.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest token audit logs. Track OAuth grants by application and scope. Identify high-risk scopes (full Drive access, Gmail read). Alert on new third-party apps accessing sensitive scopes. Report for governance review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gws sourcetype="gws:token" event_name="authorize"
| stats dc(actor.email) as unique_users by app_name, scope
| sort -unique_users
```

Understanding this SPL

**Third-Party App Access** — OAuth app grants to third-party applications create data access risks. Monitoring enables governance and risk assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: Google Workspace token audit log. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_GoogleWorkspace`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gws; **sourcetype**: gws:token. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=gws, sourcetype="gws:token". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by app_name, scope** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (third-party apps with scope), Bar chart (apps by user count), Pie chart (scope distribution).
visualization: Table (third-party apps with scope), Bar chart (apps by user count), Pie chart (scope distribution).
